citations.note: Permission is granted to anyone to use this software for any purpose, including commercial applications
to alter it and redistribute it freely
Permission is granted to anyone ... to alter it and redistribute it freely
Permission is granted to anyone to use this software for any purpose
Clause 3: This notice may not be removed or altered from any source distribution

conditions.commentary: The retention obligation is scoped to source distributions only; binary distributions are not required to include the notice.

citations.note: Clause 2: Altered source versions must be plainly marked as such, and must not be misrepresented as being the original software

conditions.commentary: Clause 1 says an acknowledgment in product documentation 'would be appreciated but is not required' (s-6), and clause 3 only requires the notice to be preserved in source distributions (s-10). Binary redistribution carries no attribution obligation.

citations.note: In no event will the authors be held liable for any damages arising from the use of this software
This software is provided as-is, without any express or implied warranty
Clause 1: The origin of this software must not be misrepresented; you must not claim that you wrote the original software
Clause 2: Altered source versions ... must not be misrepresented as being the original software

limitations.commentary: No express trademark grant. Clauses 1 and 2 create an anti-misrepresentation rule functionally similar to the BSD-3 'no endorsement' clause: you may neither claim authorship of the original nor pass off a modified version as the original.
